dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 6168 IH: Airport TIFIA Financing Certainty Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Hurd of Colorado (for himself and Mr. Garamendi) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure

form.official-title: To redefine the eligibility for airport-related TIFIA projects, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Airport TIFIA Financing Certainty Act.

subsection.text: Section 601(a)(12)(G) of title 23, United States Code, is amended to read as follows:

subparagraph.text: any project that constructs new or improves existing aviation-related facilities, including equipment, regardless of revenue producing purposes or public accessibility, that facilitates, preserves, enhances, or expands—

clause.text: access to air transportation, including surface transportation, rental car, and parking facilities;
the movement of passengers, baggage, or cargo; or
the safety or security of airport users; and

subsection.header: Determination of eligibility and project selection
subsection.text: Section 602(a) of title 23, United States Code, is amended—

paragraph.text: in paragraph (3) by striking A project and inserting Except for a project described in section 601(a)(12)(G), a project; and
in paragraph (5)(B)(iii) by striking eligible project costs and inserting a Federal credit instrument.

subsection.text: Section 603(b)(6) of title 23, United States Code, is amended by adding at the end the following:

subparagraph.text: The conditions described in subparagraph (B)(i)(III) and subparagraph (6)(B)(ii)(I) shall not be required for a project described in section 601(a)(12)(G) to receive a waiver under this paragraph.

subsection.text: Section 605(f)(1) of title 23, United States Code, is amended by striking eligible project costs that are reasonably anticipated not to equal or exceed $75,000,000 and inserting a TIFIA loan amount that is reasonably anticipated not to equal or exceed $100,000,000.
